title: external tools - telnet not work from mremoteng although it works well with standard CMD
author: longtth
body_snippet:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->

add an external tools with 

- Display name: telnet
- Filename: `CMD`
- Arguments: `/k telnet %HOSTNAME% %PORT%`  ​
​
## Expected Behavior

mremoteng shoudld display CMND windows with telnet result 

## Current Behavior

although I could run telnet successfully with standard CMD, when I try with mremoteng it throws error that `'telnet' is no recognized as an internal or external command operable program or batch f...

iterations.description: Telnet.exe fails to launch from mRemoteNG because the argument parsing logic for non-batch external tools incorrectly removes quotes, preventing telnet.exe from interpreting arguments with spaces correctly.
Bulk verified: commit a1717026 exists (MEDIUM confidence)

comments.author: LIyoai
comments.snippet: Try C:\Windows\Sysnative\telnet.exe instead of just telnet

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

**Commit:** `a1717026` (fork `robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`)
**What changed:** external tools - telnet not work from mremoteng although it 

A beta build (v1.81.0-beta.4) including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Ou...

notes:
AI triage (gemini): Telnet.exe fails to launch from mRemoteNG because the argument parsing logic for non-batch external tools incorrectly removes quotes, preventing telnet.exe from interpreting arguments with spaces correctly.
Approach: Modify SetProcessProperties in ExternalTool.cs for non-batch, non-elevated processes to use process.StartInfo.Arguments = parsedArguments; instead of splitting arguments and adding them to ArgumentList. This ensures that the argument string (including any necessary quotes) is passed directly to the external tool.
